claim: Corpus characterization: 18 deposits decompose into three interlocking programs A (PCF arithmetic stratification 7+ deposits), B (BT/Newton-polygon backbone 2 deposits), C (AI Epistemic Governance Stack 5+ deposits P7-P11)
Impact gap 1: flagship theorem split across 7+ deposits creating non-trivial assembly burden on potential citers
Impact gap 2: Painlevé bridge (V_quad -> P_III(D6)) is most striking + least developed deposit; tribe-crossing claim (Painlevé/integrable-systems/transcendence/modular-forms)
Impact gap 3: Desert stratum ~95.7% at D=4 is genuinely surprising + underexploited; lives inside (4,2) paper without standalone identity

Move 1 RECOMMENDED: consolidate flagship preprint ~40-60 pages with 7 specific content slots, target top general-math journal (Inventiones/Duke/IMRN/Compositio/JAMS); other deposits become supplements

Move 2 RECOMMENDED: Painlevé bridge as own paper, target Painlevé community via Nonlinearity/Constructive Approx/J.Integrable Systems/CMP; venue strategy: OPSFA/SIDE meetings not number-theory journals

Move 3 RECOMMENDED: Desert stratum standalone empirical paper (proposed title 'Generic Transcendence in Polynomial Continued Fractions: A 95.7% Desert at Degree 4'); targets LMS J.Comp.Math / Math.Comp sisters / Annals of Combinatorics / Ramanujan Journal
Move 4 RECOMMENDED: methodology paper 'SIARC Methodology: Case Study from PCF' bridges Programs A/B + C; target Notices/Bulletin AMS / EMS Newsletter / Nature-Science commentary

Move 5 RECOMMENDED: community-building via (a) open-problems doc on arXiv with bounty-style statements (b) GitHub reproducibility notebooks + binder/colab (c) personalized outreach 5-10 researchers in Adamczewski/Bugeaud + Joshi-Mazzocco-Clarkson + Costin-Sauzin-Mitschi + Bailey-Borwein orbits

Move 6 RECOMMENDED: institutional affiliation via (a) conference talk JMM 2027/ICMS/OPSFA/Painlevé biennial (b) co-authored paper with established researcher; single co-authored paper > ten solo submissions for credibility calibration

TABLE recommendation: umbrella revision churn (v2.0 -> v2.2 -> v2.3 standalone Zenodo deposits) is provenance-churn diluting citation graph; convert to bridge living-document with major-content-milestone snapshots only
ASSERTION: Programs A + B have enough closed results to support flagship paper RIGHT NOW; Route F closure strengthens Move 2 but NOT required for Moves 1/3/4/5/6
